seo.title: Arizona Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Arizona — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license arizona, dental assistant certification arizona, RDA arizona, DANB CDA arizona, dental assistant radiography arizona

board.name: Arizona State Board of Dental Examiners
board.mailing_address: 1740 W Adams St, Suite 2400, Phoenix, AZ 85007

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in Arizona.

steps.title: Review Expanded Functions Requirements
steps.description: Thoroughly review the Arizona State Board of Dental Examiners (ASBDE) website, particularly the 'Dental Assistant' section and the 'Dental Assistant Expanded Functions Reference Guide,' to understand the specific training, education, and certifications required for each expanded function you intend to perform.

steps.title: Complete Training and Obtain Certifications
steps.description: Successfully complete all necessary board-approved training programs and obtain required certifications, such as CPR/BLS and specific certifications for each expanded function (e.g., Coronal Polishing, Pit and Fissure Sealants, Nitrous Oxide Monitoring), as outlined by the ASBDE.

steps.title: Gather Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Compile all required supporting documentation, which may include official transcripts, course completion certificates, Dental Assisting National Board (DANB) certifications, and current CPR/BLS cards, to demonstrate fulfillment of all expanded function notification requirements.

steps.title: Create an Online Account
steps.description: If you do not already have one, create a new applicant account on the ASBDE Online Services Portal (elicense.az.gov) by clicking 'Create Account' and providing the requested personal and login information.

steps.title: Submit Expanded Function Notification
steps.description: Log in to your account on the ASBDE Online Services Portal (elicense.az.gov), navigate to the appropriate section for dental assistant expanded function notifications, complete the electronic form, and upload all prepared supporting documents for review by the Board.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Arizona does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does Arizona require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, Arizona does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Arizona?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Arizona.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Arizona?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Arizona?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Arizona. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: Arizona does not license general dental assistants. Certification required for expanded functions like radiography and coronal polishing.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Arizona
hero.dek: Dental assistants in Arizona seeking to perform expanded functions must submit their notification, along with all supporting documentation, through the Arizona State Board of Dental Examiners' ASBDE Online Services Portal (elicense.az.gov) after meeting specific training requirements.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to elicense.az.gov, click 'Create Account' from the left-hand menu, and follow the prompts to enter your personal information and create your login credentials.
